mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending corporate margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day returns, or the mechanism shows positive predictive power in early-cycle expansions instead.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, higher discount rates compress equity valuations and reduce risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding economic output and corporate earnings growth, directly generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 10-year sample, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing inflation during a post-shock recovery phase indicates resolving supply constraints and improving profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels above 110 do not precede positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation accelerates concurrently.
